payload.summary: Accept fr-19-b035a585 and fr-19-4993d634 for real attempt-identity collision bugs. Reject fr-19-b353e532 because blanket removal of started_at fallback logic breaks existing repair and idempotency paths without providing a replacement identifier.

votes.rationale: The fallback path at lines 476-479 of matching_finalized_task_runs filters only by started_at after the exact run_id match fails. When the caller provides run_id=Some("run-3") and a finalized entry exists with run_id=Some("run-2") sharing the same started_at, the fallback incorrectly returns that entry, blocking a legitimate new named run. The intended purpose of the fallback is to catch the runless-to-named upgrade case (an entry with run_id=None that was started at the same time). Adding entry.run_id.is_none() to the fallback filter is a targeted, correct fix that preserves the legacy upgrade path while preventing cross-contamination between two distinct named runs.

The started_at fallback is the sole correlation key for legacy and runless attempts across same_attempt (line 307), legacy_group_accepts_completion (line 554), find_matching_running_task_run, and the record_task_run_start idempotent replay path. Removing it wholesale would break: (1) idempotent start replay for runless entries after partial writes, (2) pairing of runless start entries with later terminal entries during collapse, and (3) the backfill path that upgrades a running runless entry to a named one. The timestamp-collision concern is valid but is better addressed by fr-19-b035a585's targeted fix and by introducing a durable attempt identifier in a future change, rather than by ripping out the only existing correlation mechanism with no replacement.

repairable_completion_event matches on timestamp+event_type+bead_id, then CompletionJournalDetails::merge treats a None run_id as compatible with any run_id value (lines 2299-2301 only reject when both are Some and differ). This means two genuinely distinct attempts for the same bead completing at the same timestamp—where one lacks a run_id—will be merged into a single event, silently losing audit history for one attempt. The dedupe key needs to incorporate real attempt identity (run_id or at minimum the full details including started_at of the originating attempt), or the repair path should refuse to merge when the incoming event represents a demonstrably different attempt.
